title: Versioned Migrations, Expand-Contract Changes, and Rollback Evidence · Go Semantics Case: Feed Go Expand Contract
summary: Developers edit production tables manually, migrations mix irreversible data loss with schema changes, deploys assume zero old binaries, and rollback has never run. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go expand contract increment in rehearsed schema and data migration chain. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, chan…
objectives: Stage additive schema, backfill, dual-read or dual-write, validation, cutover, cleanup, and old-version compatibility.
Decide transaction boundaries from each PostgreSQL operation and separate concurrent index, long data, and lock-sensitive work.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Expand Contract · Theory Feed Go Expand Contract
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 6817 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove immutable migrations, expand-contract compatibility, bounded backfill, and recovery; the release claim must bind source, migrations, dependencies, binary, image, configuration, and restored state; then change one schema, constraint, transaction, upsert, migration, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

checks.description: The cumulative go portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker // Evidence: feed-go-expand-contract-fdat2a.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: feed-go-expand-contract-fdat2a(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?func\s+evidence_feed_go_expand_contract_fdat2a\s*\([^)]*\)\s*\(bool,\s*int,\s*string\)\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?migrationCases < 7)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?resumedBackfills < 2)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?compatibilityVersions < 2)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?unreconciledRows > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using stage and additive plus specific case evidence.
